question:
What is the core tension that makes "radical honesty" — saying everything you think without filter — controversial as a social value?
A) Most people are unable to physically hear complete honesty.
B) Honesty is only appropriate when delivered in a formal setting.
C) While truth-telling is generally valued, unfiltered honesty can disregard the harm words cause, prioritizing the speaker's comfort over the listener's well-being.
D) Radical honesty guarantees that all relationships will improve permanently.
